title: Activity dark mode and commit accordion refinement
summary: EVE UI no longer marks the latest snapshot as an earlier product version, renders the provided eve.svg as a full wordmark in navigation and repository headers, uses dark-mode tokens on activity surfaces, and replaces the duplicated repository Activity timeline with expandable activity rows that show GitHub-linked snapshot commits.
userVisibleChange: Users see a clean latest snapshot page, an uncropped eve logo, consistent dark activity screens, and a repository Activity tab where recent snapshot rows expand to reveal clickable GitHub commit messages.

risks.title: Older records may carry broader implementation commit arrays
risks.mitigation: The accordion filters commit display to the snapshot Git state and falls back to a generated row only when commit metadata is unavailable.

risks.title: Dark mode still depends on global utility overrides
risks.mitigation: Expanded coverage for fractional slate backgrounds, slate text, and dark slate surfaces used by the current activity screens, and verified computed dark-mode colors in browser.

timeline.title: Fixed latest snapshot warning and logo rendering
timeline.summary: Suppressed the earlier-version badge when the route is the latest snapshot and replaced cropped logo treatments with the full eve.svg wordmark.

timeline.title: Refined activity dark mode
timeline.summary: Moved activity page backgrounds to design tokens and expanded dark-preview utility overrides for the classes used by activity dashboards and accordions.

timeline.title: Built repository activity accordion
timeline.summary: Replaced the duplicated Activity tab timeline with expandable recent activity rows that reveal snapshot commit messages and GitHub commit links.

timeline.title: Verified automated checks and browser behavior
timeline.summary: Ran UI tests, Go tests, production UI build, whitespace checks, and Playwright checks for latest snapshot copy, logo text, dark activity colors, and commit accordion links.

decisions.title: Suppress earlier-version copy on the latest snapshot route
decisions.rationale: The previous check compared the checked-out Git state with the latest Git state, which could warn on the latest snapshot; comparing the route id with the latest snapshot id matches user expectations.

decisions.title: Use the provided eve.svg as the displayed wordmark
decisions.rationale: The asset already contains the eve typography, so wrapping it in a square icon crop and adding text separately made the logo look broken and duplicated.

decisions.title: Replace duplicated repo Activity timeline with an accordion
decisions.rationale: The Activity tab was showing the same recent activity and timeline concepts together, so expanding recent rows in place gives the tab a distinct purpose while keeping commit details close to each snapshot.

decisions.title: Link the accordion to the snapshot Git state commit
decisions.rationale: Snapshot records may include historical implementation context, while the UI summary treats each snapshot as one Git state; filtering to that Git state keeps the displayed commit count and links coherent.

validation.output: Vitest ran src/format.test.ts with 3 passing tests.
tsc --noEmit, vite build, and embedded UI copy completed successfully; Vite reported the existing chunk-size warning.

Verified latest snapshot has no earlier-version banner, brand link has no extra text, dark preview uses dark body background, and repo Activity accordion shows one GitHub-linked commit for the latest snapshot.
